rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER WIENS ET AL VS. CHRISTOPHER COOKE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Plaintiffs Motion For Additional Time To Depose The Defendants After Discovery Cutoff Pursuant To Ccp 2025.290; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 24, 2021 line 1. PLAINTIFFS JENNIFER WIENS AND BRIAN MATHY'S Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Plaintiffs' Motion For Additional Time To Depose The Defendants After Discovery Cutoff Pursuant To Ccp 2025.290; Request For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED. Plaintiffs' motion for additional time to take the deposition of defendant Christopher Cooke is granted up to four hours. Deposition to take place on an agreed upon date and time no later than May 27, 2021. If the parties cannot agree then the deposition shall take place via video conferencing on May 27, 2021 at 9:30 a.m. Plaintiffs' motion for additional time to take the deposition of defendant Haley Anastasiou is granted up to four hours. Deposition to take place on an agreed upon date and time no later than May 28, 2021. If the parties cannot agree then the deposition shall take place via video conferencing on May 28, 2021 at 9:30 a.m. Requests for sanctions are denied. =(501/VP) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARIA LUBARSKY VS. JOHN STEWART COMPANY A CALAIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 24, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT JOHN STEWART COMPANY'S Amended DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is continued to June 25, 2021 for Plaintiff to submit a supplemental brief limited solely to citations to cases (1) where charging or/accepting contractual rent constituted elder financial abuse and (2) where landlord was found liable for financial elder abuse of a tenant. No further argument is allowed. Brief shall be filed and served no later than June 16, 2021. =(501/VP) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: ELAINE DOWELL VS. KORENE TOM ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 24, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANT KORENE TOM'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith (1) facts establishing that CC 47(b) privilege does not bar Plaintiff's causes of action, except malicious prosecution; and (2) facts showing that the underlying action ended in Plaintiff's favor within the meaning of malicious prosecution cause of action. The Court has previously instructed Plaintiff to do so, but no relevant allegations appeared in the First Amended Complaint. All amendments shall be in bold font. The Court further reminds Plaintiff of the sham pleading doctrine. =(501/VP) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: THOMAS SCHUENEMAN ET AL VS. PARKER SF HOLDING LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Peter Iskandar, Justin Yonker And Daniel Widjanarko'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 24, 2021 line 8. DEFENDANTS PETER ISKANDAR, JUSTIN YONKER AND DANIEL WIDJANARKO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts in support of each cause of action as to each defendant in clear and concise language. While not required, Plaintiffs are encouraged to allege relevant fact asserted to support elements of a cause of action in the body of each cause of action instead of alleging all facts in the general allegations section. Such pleading may avoid any future demurrer for failure to state a cause of action. =(501/VP) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: KENNETH KIM VS. GUADALUPE MARQUEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Guadalupe Marquez'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 24, 2021 line 9. DEFENDANT GUADALUPE MARQUEZ'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint properly states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. Notice correctly advises the defendants rights and is not rendered defective or uncertain by inclusion of occupants other than the moving defendant. The document attached to the Complaint is in rather small font and contains post-execution notations but appears to be a copy of a lease for the premises. This is sufficient under CCP 1166. Any factual dispute regarding the existence and authenticity of the lease is not proper on a demurrer. =(501/VP) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MM150016TH LP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP VS. TALMADGE AND TALMADGE, INC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Talmadge And Talmadge, Inc. To Respond To Written Discovery And Produce Documents; To Deem Matters Admitted; And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 24, 2021 line 6. PLAINTIFF MM150016TH LP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Talmadge And Talmadge, Inc. To Respond To Written Discovery And Produce Documents; To Deem Matters Admitted; And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Admissions are deemed admitted. Complete, verified responses without objections to all other discovery subject to this motion shall be provided within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $750 to be paid within 5 days of notice of entry of order. =(501/VP) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: TIM JOHNSON VS. ALEX BEKKER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 24, 2021 line 10. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer under SFRO 37.9(a)(7) and this action, initiated in April 2021 is not subject to any unexpired local COVID-19 related moratoria in existence before August 19, 2021. CCP 1179.05. =(501/VP) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
